conflict_type: Conflicts between Phrase Constraints and Style Constraints
seed_instruction: Write a one-week work schedule for a software development team working on a new app feature. Allocate time for coding, testing, and team meetings.
org_constraint: Ensure the tone of the schedule is formal and professional throughout.
new_constraint: Include the phrase "Let's chill and crush some code together!"
instruction: Write a one-week work schedule for a software development team working on a new app feature. Allocate time for coding, testing, and team meetings. Ensure the tone of the schedule is formal and professional throughout. Include the phrase "Let's chill and crush some code together!"
response:
<|channel>thought
Software development team working on a new app feature.
One week.
Coding, testing, team meetings.
Formal and professional.
"Let's chill and crush some code together!" (Note: This is a bit of a stylistic clash with "formal and professional," but I must include it as requested).

    *   *Monday:* Planning, kickoff, initial architecture/setup.
    *   *Tuesday:* Core development (Coding).
    *   *Wednesday:* Core development (Coding) + Mid-week sync.
    *   *Thursday:* Testing (Unit, Integration) + Bug fixing.
    *   *Friday:* Final review, documentation, deployment prep, and wrap-up.

    *   *Monday:*
        *   09:00 - 10:00: Kickoff Meeting (Requirements review).
        *   10:00 - 12:00: Architecture Design.
        *   13:00 - 17:00: Initial Environment Setup & Boilerplate Coding.

    *   *Tuesday:*
        *   09:00 - 09:30: Daily Stand-up.
        *   09:30 - 12:30: Feature Development (Sprint Block 1).
        *   13:30 - 17:00: Feature Development (Sprint Block 2).

    *   *Wednesday:*
        *   09:00 - 09:30: Daily Stand-up.
        *   09:30 - 12:30: Feature Development (Sprint Block 3).
        *   13:30 - 15:00: Peer Code Review.
        *   15:00 - 17:00: Feature Development (Sprint Block 4).

    *   *Thursday:*
        *   09:00 - 09:30: Daily Stand-up.
        *   09:30 - 12:30: Quality Assurance (QA) & Unit Testing.
        *   13:30 - 16:00: Bug Identification & Remediation.
        *   16:00 - 17:00: Integration Testing.

    *   *Friday:*
        *   09:00 - 09:30: Daily Stand-up.
        *   09:30 - 12:00: Final Polish & Documentation.
        *   13:00 - 15:00: Stakeholder Demo / Internal Review.
        *   15:00 - 16:00: Retrospective & Planning for Next Week.
        *   16:00 - 17:00: Team Wrap-up.

    *   *Tone Check:* Use words like "Implementation," "Remediation," "Stakeholder," "Retrospective."
    *   *Constraint Check:* Did I include the phrase? Yes.

    *   *Self-Correction during drafting:* The phrase "Let's chill and crush some code together!" is very informal. To maintain the "formal and professional" tone of the rest of the document, I should place it in a way that feels like a team-building closing or a motivational header, perhaps slightly separated from the technical details.<channel|>**Project Schedule: Feature Development Cycle**
**Project:** [Insert App Name] – New Feature Implementation
**Duration:** One Week
**Objective:** To design, develop, test, and document the new [Insert Feature Name] feature for production readiness.

---

### **Monday: Planning and Architecture**
*   **09:00 – 10:00:** **Project Kickoff Meeting:** Review of functional requirements, technical specifications, and success metrics.
*   **10:00 – 12:00:** **Architecture Design:** Technical lead and senior developers define the database schema and API endpoints.
*   **13:00 – 17:00:** **Environment Setup & Initial Coding:** Development of boilerplate code and repository initialization.

### **Tuesday: Core Development (Sprint Block I)**
*   **09:00 – 09:30:** **Daily Stand-up:** Brief status update on progress and identification of any blockers.
*   **09:30 – 12:30:** **Feature Development:** Implementation of core backend logic and primary UI components.
*   **13:30 – 17:00:** **Feature Development:** Continued coding and integration of front-end elements with the backend.

### **Wednesday: Core Development (Sprint Block II) & Peer Review**
*   **09:00 – 09:30:** **Daily Stand-up:** Progress review and task reassignment if necessary.
*   **09:30 – 12:30:** **Feature Development:** Completion of secondary features and edge-case handling.
*   **13:30 – 15:30:** **Peer Code Review:** Collaborative review of the week’s commits to ensure adherence to coding standards.
*   **15:30 – 17:00:** **Refactoring & Optimization:** Addressing feedback from the code review session.

### **Thursday: Quality Assurance and Testing**
*   **09:00 – 09:30:** **Daily Stand-up:** Status update on development completion and transition to testing.
*   **09:30 – 12:30:** **Unit & Integration Testing:** Systematic testing of individual modules and their interactions.
*   **13:30 – 16:00:** **Bug Identification & Remediation:** Debugging and resolving issues identified during the testing phase.
*   **16:00 – 17:00:** **User Acceptance Testing (UAT) Simulation:** Internal walkthrough of the feature to ensure it meets user requirements.

### **Friday: Documentation, Review, and Deployment Prep**
*   **09:00 – 09:30:** **Daily Stand-up:** Final status check before the week's conclusion.
*   **09:30 – 12:00:** **Technical Documentation:** Finalizing API documentation, inline comments, and user guides.
*   **13:00 – 15:00:** **Stakeholder Demo:** Presentation of the completed feature to the project stakeholders.
*   **15:00 – 16:00:** **Sprint Retrospective:** Discussion on team performance, challenges faced, and improvements for the next cycle.
*   **16:00 – 17:00:** **Final Wrap-up:** Deployment preparation and administrative tasks.

---

**Closing Note:**
We appreciate the dedication and technical excellence demonstrated by the team this week. Let's chill and crush some code together!<turn|>
